batches.expectedOutcome: The repository no longer overclaims parity, placeholder integration tests are replaced with meaningful coverage, and daemon/runtime docs match verified behavior.
batches.goal: Align README/docs and integration coverage with what the product actually does today, while removing the most misleading placeholders.
batches.name: Batch 1 - Honest product surface and integration hardening

batches.expectedOutcome: Supported execution paths are clearly usable through the shim and CLI, unsupported interactive paths are explicitly surfaced, and daemon/shim integration has real end-to-end coverage.
batches.goal: Make the actual shim/daemon execution path more usable and explicit for supported scenarios, especially around PowerShell command routing and installation/status behavior.
batches.name: Batch 2 - Shim and daemon usability completion
verification: manual CLI smoke tests for shim/exec flows on the current platform

batches.expectedOutcome: Platform capability reporting and enforcement behavior become less scaffolded, and policy-backed security guarantees are either implemented or explicitly downgraded in docs.
batches.goal: Expand the next realistic enforcement slices beyond current prechecks and Windows job control, while keeping behavior testable and honest.
batches.name: Batch 3 - Enforcement depth and policy-backed behavior

batches.expectedOutcome: Cross-platform install, CI, release, and Docker guidance are accurate and backed by the build/test matrix the repo can really support.
batches.goal: Tighten the packaging, install scripts, Docker surface, and CI stories so they match the actual runtime state across OS targets.
batches.name: Batch 4 - Cross-platform and packaging truthfulness
verification: workflow lint or dry-run checks where available
dotnet publish checks for documented RIDs

batches.expectedOutcome: The repo either reaches an honest high-parity state or documents the exact blockers that still prevent saying it fulfills the entire spec.
batches.goal: Reassess remaining gaps against request.task.md and either implement the last high-impact items or reduce claims so the project description is truthful.
batches.name: Batch 5 - Final parity closure
targets: remaining code hotspots identified by earlier batches
verification: full solution build and test run
manual end-to-end validation of the supported product surface

risks: The requested spec is broader than the current repository maturity, so some remaining gaps may require reducing documentation claims before true feature completion is possible.
Linux and macOS enforcement layers likely cannot reach full agentsh-style parity in one pass without native helpers or kernel integrations that do not yet exist here.
Shim and harness behavior may expose hidden integration bugs once the current unit-tested code paths are exercised end-to-end through the real binaries.
Existing docs such as docs/final-report.md may conflict with verified behavior and need coordinated correction to avoid false confidence.
successCriteria: The repository builds and tests cleanly with meaningful integration coverage for the supported execution surface.
Documentation claims match verified behavior instead of architectural aspiration.
The highest-impact usability and enforcement gaps identified in the audit are either implemented or explicitly documented as remaining blockers.
A final parity review can justify the completion claim with specific evidence rather than placeholders or scaffolding.
